observations.recommendation: 3. Conduct the complete physical count and prepare and submit a consolidated Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RCPPE).

5. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies with that of the RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper balances of RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

8. We recommended and management agreed that management utilize the LDRRMF only for expenditures that are directly related to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, and rehabilitation and recovery in adherence to Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121 and Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

11. Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.

13. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the timely implementation of all 20% Development Projects by instructing the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the projects and at the same time to provide specific target dates of implementation in the Revised/ Annual Investment Plan (AIP).

15. Instruct the Inspection Office or Inspectorate Team to furnish copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely validation/inspection thereof, pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.

17. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to inform the administrator of the e-TRACS software on the errors noted and request for immediate correction to avoid unreliable and misleading reports.

18. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the appraisal committee to evaluate the condition of these vehicles and determine their fair value or nominal value, whichever is available and the municipal accountant to record the same based on the report of the appraisal committee.

19. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor to exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Certificate of Title of Transfer (TCT) or other evidence satisfactory to it that the title is in the Municipality of Sikatuna pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445 and ensure that a budget is allocated for this purpose.

20. We recommended that the municipal mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person through the Municipal Planning and Development Office to establish GAD database to concretely define its gender issues, and accordingly design programs and activities that could most address them and ensure that planned gender responsive programs/activities are implemented in order to fully integrate gender and development in the operations of the municipality.

21. We recommended that the liquidation reports, vouchers and MOA of projects amounting to ₱36,912,699.00 be submitted for review/audit including those projects that have been partially paid and not yet started.
23. Identify the Obsolete/Unserviceable or Scrap Materials; and
24. Create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the value of said properties. Also, facilitate the immediate disposal of these properties pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445.

26. We recommended that management follow the pertinent rules and regulations set forth by the CSC in the hiring of contract of service/job order personnel. Further, to return the difference of the salaries amounting to ₱94,470.68 as computed in the table above.

1.7. We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to restore in the books the properties de-recognized without prior authority from
COA totaling ₱8,505,516.46 to ensure the fair presentation of the year-end
financial statements.
1.8. We further recommended that management comply with the proper procedures
for the one-time cleansing of its PPE account balances which includes the de-
recognition of non-existing / missing PPEs without available record of
accountability prescribed in Circular No. 2020-006, before proceeding to drop
the accounts from the books of the LGU.

2.5. We recommended that the designated members of Inventory Committee exert
concerted and collaborative efforts to identify and reconcile the unknown
inherited / beginning balances and, when warranted, the Municipal Accountant
to pursue de-recognizing the accounts pursuant to the procedures for the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

3.5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor call on the Sangguniang Bayan to
consider a general revision / updating of the existing Revenue Code in accordance
with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 7160, taking into account the present
business and economic environment of the municipality.

4.5. We recommended and management agreed to send a formal demand letter to the
contractor requiring the immediate return/settlement of the un-recouped
advance payment.

5.5. We recommended that the MDRRMO ensure the optimal utilization of its
LDRRM fund and that the programs, projects and activities related to disaster
prevention, mitigation and preparedness be implemented within the scheduled
timeframes to maximize the benefits that can be derived therefrom.

6.7. We recommended and management agreed to stop charging its LDRRM fund
with expenditure items that are outside its coverage pursuant to Item 4.3 of
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and adhere to Section 5.0 of
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 which defines and limits the utilization
of the fund to four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management.

7.6. We recommended and management agreed that the municipal officials involved
in the planning, programming and procurement of projects to be funded out of
the 20% Development Fund ensure that the same are procurement-and-
implementation ready pursuant to Item 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1
thereby guaranteeing the prompt delivery of much needed services to the
community.

8.5. We recommended and management agreed to process petty cash payments using
the petty cash vouchers pursuant to Section 48, Chapter 3 of the New
Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual.

We recommended and management agreed that the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts be implemented pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 as follows:
1. Prepare and submit the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) for approval to the Head of the Agency to ensure that the conduct of inventory taking is properly and adequately planned;
2. Submit a copy of the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities;

4. The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the corresponding amount due and collectible for the year in conformity with the rules and procedures provided in the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

6. The Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the concerned municipal officials, maximize the use of its 20% Development Fund by dedicating it to relevant and responsive development projects, programs and activities that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
7. The Budget Officer ensure the proper charging and allocation of the 20% DF in accordance with the same DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular.

9. We recommended and management agreed to require the concerned job order and contractual personnel to prepare and submit their individual accomplishment reports, duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors, in adherence to COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure that services were actually rendered in accordance with their assigned tasks and are truly necessary to meet the service requirements of the municipality.

10. We recommended and management agreed to require the inventory committee/team to complete the conduct of the physical inventory of identified properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume1.

12. We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant incorporate in her Adjusting Entries the proper asset accounts of the mentioned transactions and record corresponding depreciation expenses based on the appropriate useful life.

14. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to furnish the Audit Team with copies of perfected contracts for infrastructure projects and purchase orders for goods procured, together with the integral parts/supporting documents thereof, within five (5) days from perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001; and

16. We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly trial balances on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.
22. Require the Inventory Committee to perform the Inventory Count;
25. We recommended and management agreed to submit an explanation and demand the contractor to refund ₱86,533.03 for the deficiencies stated above.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the appraisal committee to evaluate the condition of these vehicles and determine their fair value or nominal value, whichever is available and the municipal accountant to record the same based on the report of the appraisal committee.

We recommend that purchases of supplies and materials be recorded through asset accounts and record expenses based on duly accomplished monthly Report of Supplies and Materials issued using the perpetual inventory method as provided in the New Government Accounting System Manual. In addition, the Municipal Accountant should be required to maintain supplies ledger card containing the details for each commodity/stock and the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer maintain stock cards to account the receipts ad disposition of all supplies which reconcile with the ledger cards of the Accounting Unit.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant incorporate in her Adjusting Entries the proper asset accounts of the mentioned transactions and record corresponding depreciation expenses base on the appropriate useful life.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to ensure the timely implementation of all 20% Development Projects by instructing the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the projects and at the same time to provide specific target dates of implementation in the Revised/ Annual Investment Plan (AIP).

We recommend that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to furnish the Audit Team with copies of perfected contracts for infrastructure projects and purchase orders for goods procured, together with the integral parts/supporting documents thereof, within five (5) days from perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001. Further, instruct the Inspection Office or Inspectorate Team to furnish copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely validation/inspection thereof, pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant a report of uncollected rental fees every year-end with probability of collection to record as receivables the related income for unpaid market fees as of December 31, 2019. A proposed adjusting journal entry is provided below:
1-03-01-010  Account Receivables			₱26,300.00
4-02-02-140  Receipt from Market Operation		₱26,300.00
To record delinquent rental fees of stall occupants for CY2019 and below.
We recommend that Municipal Accountant disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of accounting records and for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor to exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Certificate of Title of Transfer (TCT) or other evidence satisfactory to it that the title is in the Municipality of Sikatuna pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445 and ensure that a budget is allocated for this purpose.
We recommend that management incorporate the procedures set forth in COA Circular 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC 2013-1 for the release and utilization of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF.

We recommend that the management consistently and strictly follow the provision provided in Department of Interior Local Government and Department of Budget Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, dated April 13, 2011, to support and finance priority development projects to attain desirable results for the community.

We recommend that the different municipal employees concerned perform their respective responsibilities in the proper recognition, monitoring and management of the Local Road Networks in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the municipal mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person through the Municipal Planning and Development Office to establish GAD database to concretely define its gender issues, and accordingly design programs and activities that could most address them. Likewise, ensure that planned gender responsive programs/activities are implemented in order to fully integrate gender and development in the operations of the municipality.

We recommend that management immediately implement the transfer of the Solid Waste Management operations in a secluded, isolated and remote area to ascertain that the public is safe from any environmental and health hazards.

We recommend that cash advances be liquidated in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. The concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances shall effect immediate settlement of the same otherwise the necessary sanction for its settlement such as the withholding of the salary to be applied to the unliquidated cash advances shall be enforced.
Furthermore, no additional cash advances shall be granted to officials and employees without liquidating the previous cash advance.

We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefits of the constituents. Otherwise, replace these with positions/items that are necessary for the proper discharge of the Municipality’s functions, targets and programs.

We recommend having the management submits an explanation on spending beyond budget. Likewise, we recommend that the Management devise and implement an updated assessment and estimate of its expenses to avoid excessive expenditure on its MOOE, thus giving the opportunity to have its fund be utilized and maximized for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: The management have followed the budget provided for the SEF.
observations.reason: It was noted in the SCBAA submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to avail of the remedies by administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action. Steps are properly enumerated in section 254 to 270 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The municipality has aggressively collected their receivables and has a current balance from GF and SEF as of December 2019 of ₱114,284.30 and ₱11,772.68, respectively.
observations.reason: It was noted on the financial position submitted.

observations.reason: As per post audit, it was noted that documents were provided for the management transactions.

Subsequent grant of travel claims contrary to the above mentioned circular shall cause suspension/disallowance of such claim.

We recommend that cash advances be liquidated in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Furthermore, no additional cash advances shall be granted to official and employees without liquidating the previous cash advance.
We recommend that management insure the Municipal properties with the Government Service Insurance System in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390.

We recommend that the liquidation reports, vouchers and MOA of projects amounting to ₱36,912,699.00 be submitted for review/audit including those projects that have been partially paid and not yet started.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with authorized depository bank as often as necessary and to avoid holding large amount of undeposited collections which exposes the risk of loss or misappropriation of funds.

There are still times that the Municipal Treasurer cannot deposit the collections daily because she is also obliged to do other works as well as attend seminars/meetings thus they have it safely kept in the in the vault.
observations.reason: It was observed during the conduct of cash examination by the team.

observations.managementAction: The management have already complied with this recommendation
observations.reason: The management has completed proper turn-over of documents.

observations.recommendation:

We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office establish the pay period for these claimants for them to claim promptly. Unclaimed amounts shall be returned to the treasury after this specific period has lapsed.

observations.reason: During the conduct of post-audit of selected payroll liquidation, it was noted that cash advances were made per category so as not to delay liquidation in case some won’t claim on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant immediately locate the unreconciled
difference in the Cash in Bank account so that the corresponding adjusting entries
can be taken up in the books. We further recommend that the Municipal
Accountant communicate to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer the errors noted
affecting the latter’s records.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant document the request for
write-off from the Commission on Audit the account balances which are considered
dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to Section 10 of COA Circular
No. 97-001. Request authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the
dormant accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries on the erroneous entry made.
We further recommend that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer be cautious in the
preparation of deposit slips to avoid these unnecessary errors.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with authorized depository bank as often
as necessary and to avoid holding large amount of undeposited collections which
exposes the risk of loss or misappropriation of funds.

We recommend that management speed up the implementation of development
projects to attain the desired benefits of socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes as intended by DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular 2011-1. Henceforth, the Municipal Planning and
Development Office and the Municipal Engineering Office closely monitor their
implementation to ensure timely completion.

We recommend strict compliance with Section 77 of PD 1445 in the
observance of proper turnover of government funds and property from outgoing to
incoming officers.

We recommend that the Disbursing Officer fully liquidate all cash advances
intact. The Office of the Municipal Accountant should refrain from accepting
partial liquidation from officials and employees.

We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office establish the pay
period for these claimants for them to claim promptly. Unclaimed amounts shall be
returned to the treasury after this specific period has lapsed.

We recommend that management stop deducting loan payments of
employees from their RATA and use these allowances as provided by law.

We recommend that management require the municipal officials to facilitate the disposal of these properties in accordance with the procedures set forth under COA Circular No. 92-386. Henceforth, forward to the Municipal Accountant the documents in the disposal as basis in dropping the cost from the books of accounts of the LGU.

Also, facilitate the immediate disposal of these properties pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation letters to different debtors and to demand payment thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management has liquidated the funds that can be identified. Henceforth, request write off of these accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 36 of PD 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management demand the contractor to refund the deficiencies.

observations.reason: An explanation for the changes in the specifications and computation that would justify a certification from the barangay regarding the project is not yet submitted by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters for the return of the unused cash advances. Withhold the salaries of these officers and employees if they fail to heed to the demand.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the Solid Waste Management operations in this unprotected area and transfer the SW operation in a secluded, isolated and remote area to ascertain that the public is safe from any environmental and health hazards.
observations.managementAction: Management has already transferred their SWM operations in the cluster landfill in Albur.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the HR-personnel or designate to establish a system of control in hiring the JO personnel which basically include the following procedures: a. Require the applicants to submit PDS, Birth or Marriage Certificates, NBI or Police Clearance and other valid proofs of identity and suitability for the job being applied for; b. Require applicants to undergo interview and other basic screening process; and c. Evaluate the performance of JO personnel and determine at the end of the contract period whether there is still necessity of renewing the job orders. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant verify the completeness of the documents before payment of wages of Job Orders.
observations.managementAction: Management requires that applicants submit the necessary documents and checks the licenses of their employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the officials ensure that the GAD funds be fully utilized and maximized for GAD programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented 67% of the GAD programs for the CY 2017.
observations.reason: Time constraint and lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate recording/adjustment in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has recorded the book reconciling items in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management restrict the Municipal Treasurer’s Office from the public by using the cashier windows to transact business with the public to safeguard government properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office is already designed to restrict access to the office personnel only. Cashier windows are already provided for taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly trial balances on the 20th day of the following month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is doing her best to comply with the submission of trial balances on the 20th day of the following month. The Municipal Accountant will submit quarterly trial balances for the CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Intensify their knowledge and competence of GAD, its goals, objectives, laws, regulations and the different gender-related issues. b. Consider as GAD PPAs only those programs and activities which address women's human rights and women's economic empowerment in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person already streamlined her GAD programs, activities and projects to address the different gender-related issues.
observations.reason: Some PPAs which are not GAD-related are still charged to the 5% GAD budget such as senior citizens and PWDs expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI). It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR 2014 and its ensuing years.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the AAPSI for the AAR 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management restrict the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office personnel to avoid incompatibility of functions and safeguard government properties.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 The temporary office still allows the taxpayers to enter the office and transact business with the public using the cashier windows to transact business. However, the Municipal Treasurer sees to it that the collections are intact and the collectors are directed to remit their collections daily.
observations.reason: The Municipal Hall is currently under rehabilitation designed to restrict the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to its personnel only.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly trial balances on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 The Municipal Accountant is doing her best to comply with the submission of trial balances on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports.
observations.reason: There is lack of manpower in the accounting department.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2013. It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR 2014 and its ensuing years.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 Management still has not submitted the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: Require the Procuring Officer to make sure that the BAC Secretariat submits the supporting documents together with the recommendation for the award of the contract to the winning bidder so that complete documents are attached to the contract upon submission to COA for review within five days from perfection hereof.
observations.managementAction: CY 2012 The Procuring Officer was instructed to submit the copies of purchase orders and contracts to COA for review within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The Procuring Office still does not submit copies of purchase orders for procurement of supplies, materials and equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the concerned municipal officials
to facilitate the disposal of these properties in accordance with the procedures set
forth under COA Circular No. 92-386. Henceforth, forward to the Municipal
Accountant the documents in the disposal as basis in dropping the cost from the
books of accounts of the LGU.

We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to
perform the Inventory Count and identify the Obsolete/Unserviceable or Scrap
Materials and create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the value of said
properties. Also, facilitate the immediate disposal of these properties pursuant to
Section 79 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to send
confirmation letters to different debtors and demand payment thereof. Henceforth,
request write off of these accounts determined to be uncollectible from the
Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 36 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management submit an explanation and demand the
contractor to refund ₱86,533.03 for the deficiencies stated above.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters for the
return of the unused cash advances. No additional cash advances be granted for
those officers and employees who failed to liquidate or return the cash advances.
Futhermore, withhold the salaries of these officers and employees if they fail
to heed to the demand.

We recommend that management stop immediately the Solid Waste
Management operations in this unprotected area and transfer the SW operation in a
secluded, isolated and remote area to ascertain that the public is safe from any
environmental and health hazards.

We recommend that management require the HR-designate to establish a
system of control in hiring the JO personnel which basically include the following
procedures:
a. Require the applicants to submit PDS, Birth or Marriage Certificates,
NBI or Police Clearance and other valid proofs of identity and
suitability for the job being applied for;
b. Require applicants to undergo interview and other basic screening
process; and
c. Evaluate the performance of JO personnel and determine at the end
of the contract period whether there is still necessity of renewing the
job orders.
We, further, recommend that the Municipal Accountant verify the
completeness of the documents before payment of wages of Job Orders.

We recommend that management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2012-002 by submitting the Report and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within
the time frame.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned employees
to abide by the above quoted provisions to give the Auditor the chance to a timely
review and evaluation of the stipulations in the Contracts, PO/JO as well as to
conduct inspection on various deliveries of supplies and equipment and to give
ample time for the Auditor to advise management on the defect, if any, in the
contract for possible correction and to ascertain the existence of delivered items and
compliance with specifications contained in the PO respectively.

We recommend that the officials concerned ensure that the GAD funds be
fully utilized and maximized for GAD programs and activities only.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate reconciling/recording/adjustment in the books the above mentioned reconciling items so as to reflect the accurate balance of the asset account.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of verifying the reconciling items since the these occurred last 2013 erroneously deposited to other bank accounts of the LGU due to entry error.
observations.reason: Some reconciling items remain unadjusted. These items include deposit reconciling errors wherein the MTO erroneously deposited the above mentioned amounts to other bank accounts of the LGU due to entry error.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct an information dissemination and tax campaign to the barangays. Furthermore, avail of the remedies by administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action as enumerated in Sections 254 to 270 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 The MTO staff located the heirs of the properties and encouraged them to pay their delinquent RPT. They also conducted Tax Information Campaign, sent demand letters, posted listings of delinquent tax payers in conspicuous places and to barangay halls and encouraged barangay officials to help disseminate the Tax Information Campaign.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of acquiring professional services thru direct contracting and adhere to the approved provisions of the latter.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 The hiring of professionals was referred to DILG and was consequently approved by DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct an AAR and submit a report with complete supporting documents to substantiate the incident. We, further, recommend that management closely monitor collections by making sure that remittances are done daily and no further request for ORs be granted without remittance of the collections of previous booklet. Furthermore, create internal control policies within the collection department such as no bringing of bags and personal money inside the collection area to avoid lost cash and ORs and even juggling of personal with public funds.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 Management has conducted an investigation and already submitted a report with complete supporting documents to substantiate the incident and their recommendation on the incident.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct all collectors and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to remit all collections daily and deposit the same not later than the next banking day, in accordance with the existing regulations. We further recommend that necessary sanctions shall be imposed, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 Bank deposits are done every other day due to the distance of the LGU from the depository bank. However, the Municipal Treasurer sees to it that the collections are intact and the collectors are directed to remit their collections daily.
observations.reason: Collectors stationed outside the Municipal Treasurer’s Office fail to remit their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management relieve the bookkeeper of her disbursing responsibility and designate another personnel who do not keep the accounting records as the disbursing officer to avoid incompatibility of functions.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 The bookkeeper was already relieved of her disbursing responsibility and another employee is already designated as disbursing officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Engineer to post notification/billboards at the project site and see to it that it is in standard form as required under COA Circular 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 Management already posted signboards for projects and are in accordance with the standard form required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Intensify their knowledge and competence of GAD, its goals, programs, activities and objectives, laws, regulations and the utilization of the GAD Budget. b. Consider as GAD PPAs only those programs and activities which address different gender issues and which address women’s human rights and economic empowerment in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 The GAD Focal Person already streamlined her programs, activities and projects to address the utilization of the GAD Budget.
observations.reason: Some PPAs which are not GAD-related are still charged to the 5% GAD budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management issue demand letters to concerned employees for them to liquidate immediately all unliquidated cash advances. No additional cash advances shall be granted to them unless the previous ones have been liquidated. Willful neglect or refusal to do the same would constitute a valid cause for the management to withhold their salaries.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 Cash Advances for travel and special purposes were immediately liquidated. No employee is granted a cash advance if he/she still has an outstanding balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create a team that will conduct the physical inventory-taking of the Municipality’s properties.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 An inventory committee and an appraisal committee were already created. However, due to lack of manpower and time constraint, the LGU still were not able to conduct the required physical inventory.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower and time constraint, the LGU still were not able to conduct the required physical inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for verification on the official use of gasoline, oil and lubricants.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 Management ensures that every trip ticket issued pertains only to official business travel and that fuel consumption is reasonable.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management maintain a detailed inventory records (Supplies Ledger Card) for each inventory item. Adhere strictly to the provisions of Sec. 04(j), Vol. I of NGAS Manual. We further recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the cost of the inventory actually remained in stock.
observations.managementAction: CY 2012 Management has created a team to conduct the physical records. However, due to lack of manpower and time constraint, the LGU still were not able to conduct the required physical inventory.
observations.reason: The LGU lacks manpower for the maintenance of inventory adjustments.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper and correct use of accounts and adhere to the provisions of the PPSAS and NGAS Manual, Volume I on the recording of inventories and its issuance.
observations.managementAction: CY 2012 Management requires the Municipal Accountant to comply with the PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. Require the Leagues concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-laws as basis of reviewing bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release. Further advise all Leagues through its officers to purchase and use the Official Receipt of Republic of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: CY 2011 The Municipal Treasurer requires the leagues to use the official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines. The Municipal Accountant monitors the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring them to submit liquidation reports. Leagues were further advised to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-laws and to use Official Receipts of the Republic of the Philippines.
